ab: kiya a hreq na waw si kisa’ qu?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: micuwa’ la?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: mikiusalan mi su’ mitaal.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: kiya a waw mu kisa’, iqaat cu pakakiya imu, iyug ku riax qu!
ch: W:我今天有事不在家，改天吧！
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: pakakiya a hreq na waw si casan qu? wahay mi su’ kumiyum i minaneq qaliyan.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: uwal, nagaaw mi su’ imu.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: pakbalayq ci magal cu kayi’ qu? papateq mu.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: uwal ga! cungus niya’.
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題。
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: pakbalayq i magal cu qalimu’ qu?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: laxi gii! aqeh ci talan, ini’ kagawi uwi ku qabqub.
ch: W:我不好看！倉庫也沒整理，不要啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: nanu’ asi ci agal cu qalimu’.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: laxi uwi ku magal cu qalimu’.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: mahuway su’ ka siwalan si kuwing i maskakaru’ ru mapaqut.
ch: M:謝謝你允許讓我採訪。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: cungus niya’, nanuwan ku papaqut su’?
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: paqucay mi su’ han, nanuwan ku waw si cu ulaqi’ su’?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: mintga kinsat kuwing cunga’, ga malax ci la kahani.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: kiya a waw su’ kahani qu?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: kiya ci matawwaw i papatasan.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: kiya i tinqalangan ragiyax uwi ku ulaqi’ su’ qu?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: ukas, kiya i qalang quwis ku ulaqi’ mu.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: isu’ nanak ku kiya i runi qu?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: suman ci cu qutux xuwil ru qutux a kabahneq, qutux mu cu imu uwi nha’.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: qutux ku ngiyaw ka saanan mu uwi, sumbabaas ikuwing, naki ti ini’ ka sapeeq mha cu.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，陪伴我，我想才不令我無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: paqucay mi su’ tikay cku Gaga’ ka papisaneq i qumaluwaap.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: ini’ kapayux a baqun mu, gi’ ini’ papbiingi cu wawwaw na qaqalup na mamaliku ku kaknayril, ga’ kiya ku baqun mu ga kalanay isu’ kahabaag.
ch: W:由於禁忌婦女碰觸男人狩獵的器具，因此我知道的不多，但我了解的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: kiya a Gaga’ mamu ka papisaneq na qaraqarax na qaqaluwaap qu?
ch: M:你們有獵具的禁忌嗎？
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: ini’ papbingiani cku kaknayril ku wawwaw na qaqalup na mamaliku.
ch: W:男士的獵具是不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: pakbalayq maglu musa’ i qumaluwaap ku kanayril qu?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: aqeq cubalay! paysan gumlu musa’ qumalup ku kanayril uwi.
ch: W:萬萬不行！女人跟著去打獵也是禁忌。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
